svg.aria-label: CAOS_SIMLAB science: the real method per family — DES, ABM, Optimization, Monte-Carlo — and the deterministic-replay contract
svg.text: DES — discrete-event
SimPy (process-interaction) + Ciw
FEL loop: pop imminent event → advance clock → run handler
M/M/c queue (S01) validated against the closed form
Wq = C(c, a)·(ρ / (1−ρ)) / (c·μ) — Erlang-C
Ciw runs 10 seeded replications as an independent second-engine cross-check.
ABM — agent-based
Mesa 3 — Agent / Model / AgentSet
each step: decide vs the start-of-step state, then apply
S02 relocate unhappy agents one-by-one (growing empty pool)
S03 flip cells synchronously · S05 fixed-order serial cascade
KPIs: segregation index S, epidemic attack rate, bullwhip ratio (variance amplification).
Optimization / OR
OR-Tools · PyVRP · NetworkX
CP-SAT job-shop: interval vars + NoOverlap (S06)
Routing VRP + PyVRP HGS head-to-head (S08)
GLOP LP: min |Σ gᵢ·xᵢ − g*·D| — blend (S11)
Haul (S07): NetworkX Dijkstra + a CP-SAT cost certificate; route flips at g*.
Monte-Carlo
joblib (threading) + SciPy stats
N independent seeded replications of the M/M/c class
the 95% CI band tightens like 1/√n as n grows (S10)
no warm-up discard — the finite-run start-up bias is the deliberate lesson.
Deterministic replay — the contract across all four families
run = f(params, seed) ⇒ the committed trace is the source of truth; the browser re-run matches it byte-for-byte.
